title: Tangy Dense Norwegian Lamb Meatballs
description: A traditional Norwegian dish featuring lamb mince, these meatballs are dense, tangy, and perfect for a light meal. Made with sugar-free friendly ingredients, they are a great option for those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine lamb mince, chopped onion, egg, breadcrumbs, salt, black pepper, nutmeg, and allspice. Mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Add kefir or buttermilk and mix until the mixture is dense and sticky.
Use your hands to shape the mixture into small meatballs, about 1 1/2 inches (3.8 cm) in diameter. You should end up with around 20-25 meatballs.
Place the meatballs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving some space between each meatball to allow for even cooking.
Bake the meatballs in the preheated oven for 18-20 minutes, or until cooked through and lightly browned on the outside.
While the meatballs are baking, prepare a tangy sauce by mixing 1/4 cup of kefir or buttermilk with 1 tablespoon of lemon juice.
Once the meatballs are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes on the baking sheet.
Serve the meatballs with the tangy sauce spooned over the top.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley or dill, if desired.
